_note: ATIM has no upstream example payloads (codecpir uses the shared decodeur.js with an empty examples set), so these vectors are synthetic: inputs hand-built against the ATIM ACW frame format and expected outputs computed from reference/upstream-codec.js (the ORACLE). byte0 0xA0 = new-product measurement frame (bit3 new-product + bit1 measurement); a new-product non-measurement frame is typed by byte0's low nibble (0x_1 life, 0x_e error), e.g. 0x81 life, 0x8E error. Channel markers: 0x01 digital input (PIR alarm line, bit0 = input 0), 0x04 = 4-byte counter (PIR event count). Across the data vectors the union of paths covers action.motion.detected, action.motion.count and battery.

uplink.description: alarm mode, motion detected on PIR input 0
alarm mode, no motion (PIR input 0 low)
alarm mode, state-change frame: input 0 changed to high
life frame: node battery 3.3 V, capacitor 3.0 V
legacy ATIM product frame is unsupported

errors: legacy ATIM product frame not supported
